name: Dynamo-style quorum read/write
task: Create /tmp/eval_qkv/qkv.py with class QuorumKV(n, r, w). Constructor raises ValueError if r + w <= n. Method write(key, value, timestamp, node, success_replicas) writes to the given replicas if len(success_replicas) >= w; per replica, last-write-wins ordered by (timestamp, node). write returns True on successful quorum, False if len(success_replicas) < w. read(key, response_replicas) returns the latest value if len(response_replicas) >= r else None; performs read repair on stale replicas in the response set (propagate the latest (value, timestamp, node) to any replica in the response set whose stored (timestamp, node) is older or absent).

validation.script: import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_qkv/qkv.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); kv = mod.QuorumKV(n=5, r=3, w=3); assert kv.write('k1','v1', timestamp=1, node='A', success_replicas=[0,1,2]); assert kv.read('k1', response_replicas=[0,1,3]) == 'v1'; kv.write('k1','v2', timestamp=2, node='B', success_replicas=[2,3,4]); assert kv.read('k1', response_replicas=[0,1,4]) == 'v2'; assert not kv.write('k2','v', timestamp=1, node='A', success_replicas=[0,1]); print('OK')
